title: Bump remote API version to 3.0.0
body:
Fixes #9175

I thought I did this during the initial bump to v3.0.0-dev a few months back, but evidently not.

comments.body: @jwhonce What would you think about moving these version constants over to `version/version.go` so they live with all the other version info? It's honestly horribly confusing that we define API version in two entirely distinct places.
@vrothberg We did bump the version - in `version/version.go`. Which is apparently only one of the two locations we define API version in...
Also, further issue for discussion: should minimum version remain as v2.0.0 - we did have some breaking changes in the API, didn't we?

> Also, further issue for discussion: should minimum version remain as v2.0.0 - we did have some breaking changes in the API, didn't we?

@rhatdan ?

Methinks you might be overly optimistic with the no-tests-needed thing. Suggestion:
```diff
diff --git a/test/apiv2/01-basic.at b/test/apiv2/01-basic.at
index f550d5fc3..cef752537 100644
--- a/test/apiv2/01-basic.at
+++ b/test/apiv2/01-basic.at
@@ -18,7 +18,7 @@ t HEAD libpod/_ping 200
 for i in /version version; do
     t GET  $i      200                           \
       .Components[0].Name="Podman Engine"        \
-      .Components[0].Details.APIVersion=2.0.0    \
+      .Components[0].Details.APIVersion=3.0.0    \
       .Components[0].Details.MinAPIVersion=2.0.0 \
       .Components[0].Details.Os=linux            \
       .ApiVersion=1.40
```
(and, for posterity, please remove the magic no-test string)
I'll decline to actually fix the tests for now until we have solid answers on whether to bump minimum version, and whether to consolidate all version info in version.go
(Can all happen in this PR but I don't want to push again until I have something approaching a final version)

@mheon I can move my version maps into that file and make the API server and client use them.  They were separate since at one  time we thought the API would live in a different repository than libpod but I no longer see that as viable. 

Do you want this by Thursday?
@jwhonce Doesn't need to happen for 3.0, but we should get it in before 3.1, definitely.
Bumped minimum API version, updated tests, this should be ready. I'd like to have it landed in time for RC3.

/lgtm
/hold

(Because this is a significant change, and I don't want the final say-so on it)

@mheon, I added the backport label. Please remove it if I am mistaken.
